last_excerpt: 1. An arch distributes the weight of what it supports by channeling it outward to the sides, using the compression of its stones to create a stable structure. 2. Each face in a crowd holds its own story, a mosaic of intentions and destinations, intersecting transiently in the shared space of a city street. Why does the scent of rain, earthy and electric, feel like a memory even when it's new?
last_concepts: the way a sandcastle holds together only while damp
a grocery bag caught in a tree
the mathematics of origami and Huzita-Hatori axioms
a fact you learned wrong and still think of first
the way a match head stores energy in potassium chlorate crystals
the way an arch distributes weight to its sides
